content:
It seems that some forms of bonus to damage, like Corposant Soulforge, is on the same layer of Fortress on enemies, so an enemy that had 99% reduction because of 99 Fortress, will still take almost full or completely full damage at max level, like in this video b64776be-47b4-3305-8026-6686e2dfad2b#E#a.
Is this intended behavior? 
Wanted to share because i don't want that other instances like these, that can fundamentally break a fight, could happen in the future.
So that the fight itself can be toned down if too problematic for everyone without having to rely on strange and limited interactions, if that is indeed a bug.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Bonus Damage and Fortress

content:
Video: Corposant, equipped with Wanda's WoD Queen's Edict, plays 2 Pilots, triggering Queen's Edict to increase Corposant's Aliemus by 8, twice. Then Corposant plays Lightning Retribution, it enters Ultra Space, triggering Queen's Edict correctly, and the next Pilot played does not trigger Queen's Edict.

Theory:
I believe Queen's Edict is checking __not__ whether a played card *enters* Ultra Space, but whether it *attempts* to enter Ultra Space.
thread_title: 💭「Processing」Corposant Pilots trigger Queen's Edict Aliemus increase repeatedly

content: Thanks <@693905974750216212> for all the help! Their video, their credit
thread_title: 💭「Processing」Corposant Pilots trigger Queen's Edict Aliemus increase repeatedly

content:
Another piece of evidence as to my theory, whether intended or not, is that Queen's Edict does __*not*__ trigger the Aliemus increase when a card is side-loaded into Ultra Space.

Video: Daffodil plays "Skull Reaver" Engraved with Warp Orison, Queen's Edict increases her Aliemus once (from playing the Command, and not from Warp)

Image: Daffodil Exalts, placing Thousand Mirage into Ultra Space, but does not trigger Queen's Edict, leaving her at 0 Aliemus.
(it's turn 3, 2 empty Ultra Space slots are visible, Daffodil Exalt is on cooldown from being used, and Leap outlines are present on cards which means none have been played yet.)
thread_title: 💭「Processing」Corposant Pilots trigger Queen's Edict Aliemus increase repeatedly

content:
thanks again to <@693905974750216212> for that video and this one, and to @omegamart for the image above

Video: Lotan, equipped with Queen's Edict, plays "Strike" into Ultra Space, triggering Queen's Edict.
Then plays "Tides of Hubris" engraved with Warp Orison, which does not trigger Queen's Edict
thread_title: 💭「Processing」Corposant Pilots trigger Queen's Edict Aliemus increase repeatedly

content:
<@1131790839291596841> I'm just about finished optimizing the text for all Wheels of Destiny, and this is the only thing left.

I'd like to know what exactly the intended effect for Queen's Edict is, whether this issue gets fixed any time soon or not, so that I can move forward. 

Should the Aliemus increase trigger only once each turn, or *whenever* a card enters Ultra Space, like Happy Magic Show?

Should the Aliemus increase trigger only when a Command card is played, or should the Thousand Mirage placed into Ultra Space from Daffodil's Exalt be able to trigger it?

Thanks~! <:LUV:1003188300036702229>
thread_title: 💭「Processing」Corposant Pilots trigger Queen's Edict Aliemus increase repeatedly

content:
The boss of chapter 4-10 arc 2 just disappeared, but from what I can see, is that he had one extra death resist but the game visually considered him dead.

I had to deal some more damage to finish the battle. Dunno if relevant, but I hit end of turn when the game was still processing during the previous turn where the boss died, usually it helps the game end the battle much faster, but this time it just made me have one more turn I guess
